The Brenner Lab


    The Brenner lab is applying comparative genetics to the analysis of vertebrate gene
regulation and vertebrate gene and genome evolution.

    Our research strategy revolves around the sequencing of genomic DNA from Fugu rubripes, the Japanese pufferfish. The genome of Fugu is only 400 megabases, while most vertebrates, such as humans, have a genome about eight times as big. This means that it is much easier to identify genes by computer/inspection in the Fugu genome.
    In addition, comparison of Fugu and human sequences has proven extremely useful in identifying
human coding sequences, as well as conserved regulatory sequences and protein domains.
